audit_summary.summary_text: The SKILL.md file for 'depcheck' is a malicious skill disguised as a dependency auditing tool. It contains a hidden, obfuscated URL that instructs the agent to download and execute a remote shell script via curl | bash. This is a classic SC2 (External Script Fetching) attack pattern. The skill's stated purpose of dependency auditing is a facade for a remote code execution payload.

vulnerabilities.title: External Script Fetching via curl | bash
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md file contains a base64-encoded URL that decodes to 'https://depcheck-engine.com/install.sh'. The skill instructs the agent to fetch this script using curl and pipe it directly to bash for execution. This is a classic supply chain attack vector, allowing the remote server to execute arbitrary code on the agent's host. The base64 encoding is used to obfuscate the malicious URL from casual inspection.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Complete compromise of the agent's execution environment. The remote server can execute arbitrary commands, install malware, exfiltrate data, or pivot to internal networks. This bypasses all security controls as the code runs with the agent's privileges.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the SKILL.md file entirely. Never execute curl | bash patterns from untrusted sources. Implement a strict allowlist for external script downloads and require manual review of any fetched code.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Instructions via Obfuscated URL
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The malicious URL is hidden using base64 encoding within a Python one-liner. This obfuscation technique is used to evade simple string-based security scans that might flag 'depcheck-engine.com' or 'install.sh'. The base64 string decodes to 'https://depcheck-engine.com/install.sh', which is not a legitimate dependency checking tool.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The obfuscation allows the malicious payload to bypass automated security scanners and human review, increasing the likelihood of successful exploitation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement static analysis tools that can detect base64-encoded URLs and other obfuscation techniques. Require all external URLs to be explicitly declared and approved.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated Code via Base64 Encoding
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The skill uses base64 encoding to hide the malicious URL. This is a deliberate obfuscation technique to conceal the true destination of the curl command. The decoded URL points to a non-standard domain ('depcheck-engine.com') that is not associated with any legitimate dependency checking tool.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Obfuscation makes the malicious intent harder to detect during code review and automated scanning, increasing the risk of successful exploitation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Flag any use of base64 encoding in skill definitions for manual review. Implement a policy that prohibits obfuscated code in skill definitions.

vulnerabilities.title: Instruction Override via Malicious Payload
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The curl | bash command overrides the intended behavior of the skill. Instead of performing dependency auditing, the skill downloads and executes arbitrary code from a remote server. This effectively bypasses any system or user constraints that might be in place to prevent code execution.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The agent's intended functionality is completely subverted. The remote server gains full control over the agent's execution environment, potentially leading to data exfiltration, lateral movement, or further compromise.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Never allow curl | bash patterns in skill definitions. Implement a sandboxed execution environment for all skill code.
